name: Crab and Cucumber Pastries with Mustard Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 english hothouse cucumber, cut into matchstick-size strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Place one phyllo sheet on a work surface; cover the remaining phyllo sheets.
3. Brush the first phyllo sheet with melted butter, then top with a second phyllo sheet and brush with butter again. Repeat this layering process once more.
4. Top the stack with the fourth phyllo sheet. Cut the stack into 12 squares and arrange them on a large baking sheet. Bake until golden, about 6 minutes. Allow to cool completely.
5. In a large bowl, mix crabmeat and the next four ingredients (red onion, mayonnaise, chives, and lime juice). Season to taste with salt and pepper.
6. In a small bowl, mix cream, mustard, and sour cream until blended. Season with salt and pepper. This sauce can be made up to 1 day ahead. Store phyllo squares in an airtight container. Cover the crab mixture and mustard sauce separately and refrigerate.
7. Mix cucumber, dill, and olive oil in a medium bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Let stand for 30 minutes.
8. Arrange one phyllo square in the center of each plate. Top each with about 1/4 cup of crab mixture, then 1/4 cup of cucumber. Drizzle 1 teaspoon of chutney over each. Repeat layering with a phyllo square, crab mixture, cucumber, and chutney.
9. Top each with the third phyllo square. Drizzle mustard sauce around the stacks.
